questions.question: The apparatus used to separate a mixture is shown. mixture funnel filter paper What is the mixture? A aqueous calcium chloride and aqueous calcium nitrate, B calcium carbonate and aqueous calcium chloride, C ethanol    and    water, D sand and calcium carbonate

options: A aqueous calcium chloride and aqueous calcium nitrate
B calcium carbonate and aqueous calcium chloride

Which terms describe the blue copper( II) sulfate and the reactions? the blue copper( II) sulfate is reactions A a mixture can be reversed, B a mixture cannot be reversed, C hydrated can be reversed, D hydrated cannot be reversed 7

B a mixture cannot be reversed

questions.question: An experiment was carried out to find the rate of reaction when 1 g of solid X reacts with 100 cm 3 of solution Y. solution Y solid X gas syringe The experiment took place too quickly for measurements to be made. questions.text: An experiment was carried out to find the rate of reaction when 1 g of solid X reacts with 100 cm 3 of solution Y. solution Y solid X gas syringe The experiment took place too quickly for measurements to be made. Which change could be made to slow down the reaction? A add a catalyst, B decrease the concentration of solution Y, C decrease the particle size of solid X, D increase the temperature
options: B decrease the concentration of solution Y
C decrease the particle size of solid X

questions.question: What   is   not a typical characteristic of acids? A They react with alkalis producing water., B They react with all metals producing hydrogen., C They react with carbonates producing carbon dioxide., D They turn blue litmus paper red. 8
questions.text: What   is   not a typical characteristic of acids? A They react with alkalis producing water., B They react with all metals producing hydrogen., C They react with carbonates producing carbon dioxide., D They turn blue litmus paper red. 8
options: A They react with alkalis producing water.
B They react with all metals producing hydrogen.
C They react with carbonates producing carbon dioxide.
D They turn blue litmus paper red. 8

questions.question: Copper( II) sulfate can be prepared by adding excess copper(II) carbonate to sulfuric acid. Why is an excess of copper( II) carbonate added? A to ensure all the copper( II) carbonate has reacted, B to ensure all the sulfuric acid has reacted, C to increase the rate of reaction, D to increase the yield of copper( II) sulfate
questions.text: Copper( II) sulfate can be prepared by adding excess copper(II) carbonate to sulfuric acid. Why is an excess of copper( II) carbonate added? A to ensure all the copper( II) carbonate has reacted, B to ensure all the sulfuric acid has reacted, C to increase the rate of reaction, D to increase the yield of copper( II) sulfate
options: A to ensure all the copper( II) carbonate has reacted
B to ensure all the sulfuric acid has reacted
C to increase the rate of reaction
D to increase the yield of copper( II) sulfate
